summary: Reconciled Control Tower operational docs and memory to the current post-auth state. Stale auth-blocker messaging was removed from active status, tasks, memory, and decisions; the rollout is now recorded as locally committed via `8d9033f06b4ff4a72589972220ddf87832fa7a78`, with remaining follow-up limited to transient artifact review, next Git handling, convention codification, and the still-ambiguous imported `auth` goal.
work_completed: Updated `.control-tower/docs/state/current-status.md`, `.control-tower/docs/state/open-questions.md`, and `.control-tower/docs/state/known-risks.md` to reflect successful auth recovery, completed Scribe and Scout curation, the local rollout commit, and the remaining post-check-in follow-up.

Replaced the stale active task with a new post-check-in follow-up task in `.control-tower/docs/tasks/active.md` and `.control-tower/state/task-ledger.json`, while marking the earlier auth-recovery and rollout-check-in work complete.

Updated `.control-tower/memory/l0.md` and `.control-tower/memory/l1.md` so working memory now reflects the local rollout commit, current conventions, remaining risks, and the narrowed open-question set.
Rewrote `.control-tower/docs/state/decisions.md` so active decisions capture the rollout conventions and local commit state, and the old auth-blocked rollout narrative is explicitly superseded.

findings.message: Operational state now records the Control Tower rollout bootstrap as committed locally on `main` via `8d9033f06b4ff4a72589972220ddf87832fa7a78` (`chore: add Control Tower rollout bootstrap`).
The repository still has untracked transient `.control-tower` artifacts after the local rollout commit, so push or PR handling should be decided only after reviewing that transient set.
Git-master and Builder conventions are now explicit in operational state, but they are not yet codified in the agent prompt or policy files.
The imported user goal `auth` remains unresolved in repo context and stays as the only open operational question.

follow_up_recommendations: Review the remaining untracked transient `.control-tower` artifacts before deciding whether to push or open a PR from commit `8d9033f06b4ff4a72589972220ddf87832fa7a78`.
Codify the Git-master branch-and-PR convention and the Builder avoid-path convention in agent guidance so the documented state matches execution policy.
Clarify the repo-specific meaning of the imported `auth` goal before routing additional scoped work from it.
memory_worthy: Control Tower rollout bootstrap is no longer auth-blocked and is recorded as a local commit on `main` at `8d9033f06b4ff4a72589972220ddf87832fa7a78`.
Operational state now treats Git-master branch-and-PR flow, Builder avoidance of live `.control-tower/` state, and `docs/` as the durable canonical docs store as active rollout guidance.
Remaining follow-up is limited to transient artifact review, next Git handling for the local commit, convention codification in agent guidance, and clarifying the imported `auth` goal.
